title: versioning — trait composition per model
description: Versioning owns 2 entities: ApiVersion + DeprecationNotice. Both compose foundation's canonical trait stack. Note that ApiVersion opts into HasVersions itself — the ApiVersion row is a versioned model, so lifecycle metadata edits (name / description / migration_target_slug rewrites) produce rollback-capable snapshots. Meta.

foundation.reason: ApiVersion + DeprecationNotice are platform-managed. is_system=true prevents tenant-side edits.
Version lifecycle transitions (release/deprecate/sunset) audit-logged for regulatory evidence + tenant DPO reports.

ApiVersion.description + migration_target_slug + notice fields may be edited over time. Row-level snapshots via mpociot/versionable let ops roll back a bad description edit or restore a corrected migration URL.
ApiVersion display order in tenant catalog + admin UI. Newer versions first by default.
ApiVersion.slug is the primary lookup key (v1, v2, 2024-10-15). Reserved slugs enforced globally.
Admin search across version names + notice text.

api_version.notes: Every ApiVersion is is_system=true — tenant admins cannot create versions. NOT BelongsToTenant — versions are platform-global (a v1 API means the same thing to every tenant). HasVersions on ApiVersion is meta — the version registry itself is versionable so ops can roll back a bad description edit.

deprecation_notice.notes: Same is_system=true policy. HasVersions on the notice body lets legal / product-ops revise notice text before publish without losing draft history. State machine: draft → published → archived. Once published, notice_markdown becomes append-only — revisions produce new versions rather than in-place edits.
